---
title: "Evaluation of Water Temperature"
format: 
  revealjs:
    embed-resources: true
---

```{r}
#| file: draft-results-code.R
#| include: false
```

# Business Problem

::: incremental
-   Power Plants face environmental constraints that affect efficiency and can lead to failure to meet demands
-   Power Plants rely on cooling to stay at a functional level
-   Global Warming elevates these constraints by making cooling systems less efficient
:::

## Context {.center}

::: fragment
Temperatures above 85°F create operation risk and forces reduced efficiency
:::

## Goal

::: incremental
-   Assess likelihood of reduced capacity
-   Evaluate the impact of running at reduced capacity
-   Project the risk and impact over the next 5 years
:::

# Data Overview

::: {.fragment fragment-index="1"}
-   NOAA water temperature data at the Dauphin Island Station
:::

::: {.fragment fragment-index="2"}
-   EIA energy generation data at the James M. Barry Electric Generating Plant
:::

::: {.fragment fragment-index="3"}
-   EIA energy demand data from the Alabama Power Company
:::

::: {.footer fragment-index="4"}
1.  NOAA (National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration)
2.  EIA (Energy Information Administration)
:::

# [Methodology]{style='color:white;' } {background-image='Station.jpg' background-size="cover"}

## Water Temperature

::: incremental
1.  Monthly Averages were calculated using confidence intervals
2.  Defined risk levels based on proportion of days above 85°F in water temperature
3.  Used linear regressions for temperature predictions
:::

## Energy Generation and Consumption

::: incremental
1.  Calculated average contribution\
2.  Energy generation was compared to consumption
3.  Evaluated impact of running at reduced capacity
:::

## Water Temperature Findings

::: {.fragment fragment-index="1"}
Peak month temperatures:
:::

::: {.fragment fragment-index="2"}
-   July *86.62°F (95% CI: 86.57-86.68)*\
-   August *86.35°F* *(95% CI: 86.31-86.38)*
:::

::: {.fragment fragment-index="3"}
High Risk months:
:::

::: {.fragment fragment-index="4"}
-   July *(72.7%)*\
-   August *(73.6%)*
:::

::: {.fragment fragment-index="5"}
Moderate Risk months:
:::

::: {.fragment fragment-index="6"}
-   June *(45.5%)*
-   September *(32%)*
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r, echo = FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(scales)
library(lubridate)


ggplot(mtempm, aes(Year, Mean_Temp, color = Month)) + 
  geom_line(aes(
            group = Month,
            color = Year > 2025)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 85) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2026) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "#0072B2", "TRUE" = "#E69F00"),
                     labels = c("Observed", "Projected")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks())+
  theme (axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1)) +
  facet_wrap(~Month) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(40, 90)) +
  labs(
    title = "Temperature over Years",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Temperature",
    color = "Status")

```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Energy Findings

::: {.fragment fragment-index="1"}
Defined Contribution: *10.3%*
:::

::: {.fragment fragment-index="2"}
Most impacted month: September of 2004 (Normal Capacity = 35.31%, 30% reduced capacity = 50.44%)
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r, echo = FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(scales)
library(lubridate)


ngen$time <- as.Date(
  paste(ngen$Year, ngen$Month, "01", sep = "-"),
  format = "%Y-%b-%d"
)



ggplot(ngen, aes(x = time, y = net_gen)) +
  geom_line(aes(alpha = pu,
                group = Capacity,
                color = Year > 2025)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(scale = 1e-6, suffix = "M")) +
  facet_wrap(~factor(Capacity)) +
  theme (axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date("2026-01-01")) +
  scale_alpha(range = c(1, 0.1)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "#0072B2", "TRUE" = "#E69F00"),
                     labels = c("Observed", "Projected")) +
  guides(alpha = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Net Generation Over Time",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Generated Megawatt Hours",
    color = "Status"
  )
```


# Interpretation


::: incremental
-   Reduced Capacity likely needed during high and moderate risk months
-   Power plant can cover reduced capacity of up to 30%
-   Failure to meet demand can be offset due to system
:::

# Limitations
::: columns
::: {.column width='50%'}
-   Missing Data
-   Missing Scope of Generation
-   Programming Errors
:::

::: {.column width='50%'}
![](AlabamaPlants.png){fig-align='center'}
:::
:::

# Recommendations

::: incremental
-   Operate at reduced capacity from June-September
-   Expand analysis to include all power plants under Alabama Power Company
-   Monitor increasing temperature and energy demand
:::

# Thank You, Questions? {.center}
